issue.title: i386 segv in     #0 0xf7a49ce0 in kj::operator*(kj::_::Stringifier, kj::Exception const&) (/lib/i386-linux-gnu/libkj-1.1.0.so+0x38ce0)
issue.body:
Not sure if we want to look into this, just leaving this here, in case someone wants to take a look. I guess it is another one of the GCC+clang mix things.

Diff to reproduce:


```diff
diff --git a/ci/test/00_setup_env_native_asan.sh b/ci/test/00_setup_env_native_asan.sh
index 2465c70..b104ddb 100755
--- a/ci/test/00_setup_env_native_asan.sh
+++ b/ci/test/00_setup_env_native_asan.sh
@@ -8,3 +8,3 @@ export LC_ALL=C.UTF-8
 
-export CI_IMAGE_NAME_TAG="mirror.gcr.io/ubuntu:24.04"
+export CI_IMAGE_NAME_TAG="mirror.gcr.io/debian:trixie"
 
@@ -21,4 +21,3 @@ fi
 export CONTAINER_NAME=ci_native_asan
-export APT_LLVM_V="22"
-export PACKAGES="systemtap-sdt-dev clang-${APT_LLVM_V} llvm-${APT_LLVM_V} libclang-rt-${APT_LLVM_V}-dev mold python3-zmq qt6-base-dev qt6-tools-dev qt6-l10n-tools libevent-dev libboost-dev libzmq3-dev libqrencode-dev libsqlite3-dev ${BPFCC_PACKAGE} libcapnp-dev capnproto python3-pip"
+export PACKAGES="systemtap-sdt-dev clang llvm libclang-rt-dev mold python3-zmq qt6-base-dev qt6-tools-dev qt6-l10n-tools libevent-dev libboost-dev libzmq3-dev libqrencode-dev libsqlite3-dev ${BPFCC_PACKAGE} libcapnp-dev capnproto python3-pip"
 export PIP_PACKAGES="--break-system-packages pycapnp"
@@ -33,3 +32,3 @@ export BITCOIN_CONFIG="\
  -DCMAKE_C_FLAGS='-ftrivial-auto-var-init=pattern' \
- -DCMAKE_CXX_FLAGS='-ftrivial-auto-var-init=pattern' \
+ -DAPPEND_LDFLAGS='-latomic' -DCMAKE_CXX_FLAGS='-ftrivial-auto-var-init=pattern' \
  -DCMAKE_EXE_LINKER_FLAGS='-fuse-ld=mold' \
@@ -38 +37,3 @@ export BITCOIN_CONFIG="\
 "
+export CI_IMAGE_PLATFORM="linux/i386"
+TEST_RUNNER_EXTRA="interface_ipc_mining.py"
diff --git a/test/sanitizer_suppressions/ubsan b/test/sanitizer_suppressions/ubsan
index ad604e6..1b9e8ab 100644
--- a/test/sanitizer_suppressions/ubsan
+++ b/test/sanitizer_suppressions/ubsan
@@ -70 +70,6 @@ shift-base:RandomMixin<*>::randbits
 shift-base:RandomMixin<*>::randbits<*>
+implicit-signed-integer-truncation:MillisToTimeval
+implicit-signed-integer-truncation:streams.cpp
+implicit-unsigned-integer-truncation:util/hasher.h
+implicit-unsigned-integer-truncation:util/hasher.cpp
+implicit-unsigned-integer-truncation:util/bytevectorhash.cpp
```

Steps to reproduce:

```
RUN_UNIT_TESTS=false MAKEJOBS="-j$(nproc)" FILE_ENV="./ci/test/00_setup_env_native_asan.sh" ./ci/test_run_all.sh
```

Output:

```

Command '['/ci_container_base/ci/test/03_test_script.sh']' returned non-zero exit status 1.

events.body:
This seems very similar the gnu32 failures I saw in https://github.com/bitcoin-core/libmultiprocess/pull/218#discussion_r2403946338 when the code was calling `kj::str(e)` on caught `kj::Exception` objects instead of calling `e.getDescription()`.

Something strange seems to be happening in the kj stringify implementation on i386 that corrupts the call stack, and this will take some effort to debug but should be doable, because this can be easily reproduced in `mptest` with small changes to https://github.com/bitcoin-core/libmultiprocess/pull/218.
